description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of metal, featuring a ring near the muzzle and a lifting handle on top. The wooden carriage is reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and it has a rectangular shape with cutouts for the wheels. The wheels are simple, solid discs, also made of metal, and are attached to axles that run through the carriage. The overall appearance is rugged and utilitarian, with visible wear and a utilitarian finish. The object is reminiscent of artillery pieces used in the 17th to 19th centuries, designed for mobility and battlefield deployment.
description: A historical metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four solid metal wheels and visible reinforcement brackets.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cannon barrel, wooden carriage, and two wheels with metal brackets.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular cannon muzzle and the alignment of all four wheels beneath the carriage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cannon barrel, carriage, and wheels, with visible reinforcement and handle details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the carriage, the breech of the cannon, and the arrangement of the wheels.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the carriage and barrel are exposed with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the object is finished in solid gray and brown tones.
